name: Crawl4AI MCP Server
what_it_does: Crawl4AI MCP server enabling AI agents to use Crawl4AI — the LLM-optimized web crawler — to scrape and extract web content in clean Markdown format, with JavaScript rendering, smart content extraction, and structured data output optimized for consumption by large language models.
use_cases: Scraping websites with JavaScript rendering from research agents
Extracting clean Markdown content from web pages for LLM processing agents
Crawling documentation sites and extracting structured content from knowledge agents
Gathering competitive intelligence from web data extraction agents
Building knowledge bases from web content via crawling agents
Extracting structured data from complex web pages for data pipeline agents
not_for: High-volume scraping at scale (consider Apify or ScraperAPI for production)
Sites requiring authentication and session management
best_when: An agent needs high-quality web content extraction optimized for LLM consumption — Crawl4AI produces cleaner Markdown output than generic scrapers, reducing noise in agent context.
avoid_when: You need simple HTML scraping without JS rendering — use lighter tools. Or if you need large-scale production crawling.

auth.notes: No authentication for the MCP server itself. Crawl4AI runs locally. Some target sites may require authentication for scraping.

pricing.paid_tiers: None for self-hosted; Crawl4AI cloud service in development
pricing.notes: Crawl4AI and this MCP are free open source. Local deployment. No external API costs.

performance.rate_limits: Local — limited by network and Playwright browser speed

known_agent_gotchas: Crawl4AI and Playwright must be installed — browser dependencies add setup complexity
JavaScript rendering is slow — budget 2-10 seconds per page
Anti-bot measures (Cloudflare, etc.) can block crawling
Community MCP wrapper around Crawl4AI — quality depends on Crawl4AI version
Respect robots.txt and site terms of service when crawling
Memory usage is high when browser instances are active
agent_readiness.security_notes: Local browser automation. No credentials for MCP. Respect ToS and robots.txt. Browser execution — sandbox appropriately. Community MCP.

security_score_components.security_notes: Browser automation locally. Respect robots.txt. Community MCP. No credentials.
